channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senators clash over flood control investigations and political accountability
item.description: Senator Alan Peter Cayetano has urged the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee (BRC) to expand its investigation into alleged anomalous flood control projects beyond Taguig City, challenging Senator Panfilo L...

item.title: Targeted Killing of 30-Year-Old Man in Plateau Community
item.description: Gunmen killed a 30-year-old man, Khalid Wahid, in his home in Dong community, Jos North, Plateau State. The attack occurred around 1am on Sunday when the attackers stormed his residence, dragged him o...

item.title: Idac oversight office addresses complaints amid scrutiny of Andrea Johnson
item.description: The Investigative Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) oversight office has confirmed receiving multiple complaints, including high-profile cases, but has not disclosed specific details about allegat...

item.title: CNN Criticizes Trump's Push for Controversial SAVE America Act, Sparking GOP Division
item.description: The article discusses how CNN host Manu Raju mocked President Donald Trump's insistence on passing the SAVE America Act, a bill targeting non-citizen voting—a practice experts say does not exist. Raju...

item.title: U.S. Justice Department's Civil Rights Division Faces Criticism Over Policy Shifts and Investigation Changes
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice’s civil rights division has come under scrutiny for its evolving approach to investigations, particularly under the Trump administration. Recent reports highlight a shif...

item.title: Arusha man's life sentence overturned after court finds rape trial flawed
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Tanzania has overturned the life imprisonment sentence of Baraka Makubi, a retail shop owner in Monduli District, after finding the rape trial flawed. The appellate panel, consi...

item.title: Hawthorn Hawks thrash lowly Richmond to climb AFL ladder
item.description: In a dominant display, Hawthorn Hawks crushed struggling Richmond by 70 points at the MCG, propelling them back into the top four of the AFL ladder. Uncontracted forward Mitch Lewis starred with five ...

item.title: TMC Leader Urges PM Modi to Hold All-Party Meeting on Delimitation and FCRA Bill Reforms
item.description: Rajya Sabha leader Derek O'Brien has written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, urging him to convene an all-party meeting to discuss the government's proposed delimitation legislation and amendments to...

item.title: The Legacy of the 1976 Montreal Olympics Boycott and Its Impact on African Athletes
item.description: The 1976 Montreal Olympics boycott by African nations against New Zealand's participation due to apartheid policies marked a pivotal moment in sports history. The article explores how athletes like Gl...

item.title: Nine killed in two road accidents in Limpopo's Sekhukhune district
item.description: Two fatal road accidents occurred in Limpopo’s Sekhukhune district on Saturday, claiming nine lives and injuring twelve others. The first crash involved a Volkswagen Polo, minibus, and truck on the R5...

item.title: Tuchel praises England squad's World Cup performance
item.description: The article discusses Tuchel's comments on England's football team, expressing hope that players will take pride in their World Cup experience. While the text provided lacks detailed content, it sugge...

item.title: Art and Social Change: How Makoko Inspired Opedun's Work
item.description: Damilola Moses Opedun, a Lagos-based contemporary artist, shares how his years of mentoring children in Makoko, Lagos' vulnerable slum, transformed his artistic practice. Through his NGO, The Seed of ...

item.title: Challenges in Philippine Journalism: Navigating Digital Transformation and Ethical Standards
item.description: The article discusses the evolving landscape of journalism in the Philippines, highlighting the challenges faced by traditional media outlets in the digital age. It emphasizes the decline of print med...

item.title: Broken Truck Causes Long Traffic Jam on Lagos Ago Road
item.description: A broken-down truck caused a severe traffic gridlock on Lagos Ago Road, trapping hundreds of vehicles and commuters for hours. The incident occurred at the SIFAX Terminal Bus Stop after the truck deve...

item.title: Trump Calls for Adding Iran to Russia Sanctions Bill, Prompting Political Backlash and Online Debate
item.description: President Donald Trump called on congressional Republicans to add Iran to pending Russia sanctions legislation following the deaths of two U.S. service members in Jordan, triggering strong political r...

item.title: Garlic Powder Recall: Bacillus Cereus Risk in Canadian Stores
item.description: The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) has issued a Class 2 recall for Heavenly Spices garlic powder sold at Dollarama stores across Canada and online due to potential contamination with Bacillus ...

item.title: Kenya Weather Forecast: Rainfall Expected in Nairobi and Several Counties Over Next 24 Hours
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has issued a forecast predicting rainfall in Nairobi and several other counties over the next 24 hours. The rain is expected to start at 9pm on Sunday, July 19, wit...

item.title: ASIC collects $830m in fines targeting major banks and corporate misconduct
item.description: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) has imposed a record $830 million in civil penalties during the past financial year, marking a significant increase compared to the previous...

item.title: Landslides in Nagaland's Mon District Kill 5, Leave 4 Missing
item.description: A series of devastating landslides triggered by heavy rainfall in Nagaland’s Mon district on Sunday resulted in at least five deaths and four people missing. Officials confirmed that search and rescue...

item.title: Retirees Return as Lifeguards in Montreal
item.description: Montreal retirees are returning to work as lifeguards, marking a significant shift in how communities address aging populations and public safety. The initiative, highlighted in a recent article, refl...
